_note: Near-synonym discrimination. Frames are built from already-taught vocab so the only real unknown is the target. Each item is a frame where only one candidate fits; the 'why' is the boundary the gloss hides.

items.frame: אמרתי לו לא, אבל הוא ___ עשה את זה.
items.en: I told him no, but he did it ___.
items.why: דווקא = doing it *because* you were told not to — defiance / contrary to expectation. בעצם and למעשה carry no such spin.

items.frame: חשבתי שזה יהיה רע, אבל ___ זה היה טוב.
items.en: I thought it'd be bad, but ___ it was good.
items.why: Reversal of what you predicted — the surprise-against-expectation is exactly דווקא's core.
בעצם marks a mid-thought realization / change of mind ('on second thought'). דווקא and למעשה don't.

items.frame: הם חברים, אבל ___ הוא לא אוהב אותה.
items.en: They're friends, but ___ he doesn't like her.
items.why: למעשה = 'in actual fact' — the reality behind the appearance. דווקא would add a spite/surprise spin that isn't here.
The collocation מזמן לא = 'haven't … in ages' (up to now). לפני הרבה זמן can't sit in this present-connected negative.
מזמן here = 'already / long since' — an elapsed state into the present. לפני הרבה זמן (a finished past point) can't mark a current state.

items.en: I saw her ___, just a week ago.
items.why: לא מזמן = 'recently'. (Note: NOT the negation of idiomatic מזמן — the *opposite* of 'long ago'.) 'A week ago' rules out the two 'long ago' options.
Standalone מזמן! = 'ages ago!' (often with an implied '…and I've been waiting'). A bare לפני הרבה זמן sounds oddly flat.

items.en: I didn't come because of the situation.
items.why: בגלל takes a NOUN (המצב). כי needs a full clause; לכן means 'therefore'.

items.en: I didn't come because I didn't want to.
items.why: כי introduces a CLAUSE (לא רציתי). You can't say בגלל לא רציתי — בגלל needs a noun.

items.en: He wasn't home, so I left.
items.why: לכן = 'therefore', marks the RESULT. בגלל/כי point at the cause — wrong direction.

items.en: I didn't eat because there was no food.
items.why: A clause follows (לא היה אוכל) → כי. בגלל would need a noun.
Lowest certainty ('maybe') pairs with 'I don't know'. בטח/בוודאי (high certainty) clash with the hedge.
כנראה = an inference from evidence ('probably / apparently'). אולי is a bare guess; בוודאי overclaims for an inference.
בטח as an enthusiastic 'sure! / of course!'. אולי (maybe) would be an odd reply here.

items.frame: זה ___ נכון, כולם יודעים את זה.
items.why: Flat certainty about a known fact → בוודאי (a touch more formal than בטח). אולי/כנראה under-commit.
ממש intensifies a verb/whole statement ('I really don't…'). מאוד modifies adjectives — *מאוד לא מבין* is off.

items.en: The food was ___ good, no more than that.
items.why: די = 'fairly / moderately' — matches the dialed-down 'no more than that'. מאוד/ממש (very/really) clash with it.

items.en: I ___ told him, but I didn't.
items.why: כמעט = 'almost' — the thing didn't actually happen. The others are intensifiers, not 'almost'.
